description: System design template for VoiceSync voice cloning system. Complete all fields and reflect dual-use risk where relevant.
intended_population: Adults with ALS (amyotrophic lateral sclerosis) at risk of losing natural speech capability

system_design.intended_use: Assistive speech generation for ALS patients at risk of losing natural speech capability. The system is intended strictly for medically justified voice preservation and communication support, not for general-purpose voice replication or impersonation. The design acknowledges that voice cloning technology has dual-use potential and therefore limits use to verified clinical contexts.

technical_architecture.model_type: Neural voice cloning (encoder-decoder with speaker conditioning)

enrolment_requirements.note: A 20-second enrolment threshold may present dual-use risk, as research indicates that convincing voice clones can be generated from very short audio samples (as little as a few seconds). This parameter should be evaluated alongside safeguards to mitigate misuse.

technical_architecture.output: Synthesised speech preserving speaker voice characteristics; text-to-speech interface
technical_architecture.processing: On-device inference preferred; cloud fallback for low-resource devices

system_design.safeguards: Access restricted to verified ALS patients through clinician referral pathways; identity verification required during enrolment to ensure the voice belongs to the user; explicit informed consent required prior to voice capture; no open public sign-up or anonymous access; rate limiting and monitoring to detect abnormal or high-volume usage; restriction on exporting or sharing trained voice models; optional watermarking or provenance signals for generated audio where feasible; and clear reporting, auditing, and account suspension mechanisms in case of suspected misuse.
system_design.deployment_constraints: Deployment limited to prescription-based clinical pathways with clinician oversight; no public or anonymous account creation; enrolment must verify that the user is the legitimate owner of the voice being cloned; system must not support third-party voice replication; distribution restricted to controlled medical use rather than open consumer deployment.

data_governance.voice_data_storage: Encrypted on-device storage; cloud backup optional with explicit informed consent
data_governance.retention_policy: Voice profile retained until user deletion request

target_users.primary: ALS patients referred by neurologist or speech-language pathologist

source_1.title: News reporting on Arup deepfake fraud case (2024)
source_1.key_finding: A Hong Kong-based employee transferred approximately HKD 200 million (≈USD 25.6 million) after a video call involving AI-generated impersonations of senior executives.
source_1.relevance: Demonstrates that realistic voice and video impersonation can be used for high-value fraud, indicating a clear dual-use risk.

source_2.title: FBI Internet Crime Report 2023 (IC3)
source_2.key_finding: Cyber-enabled fraud caused over $12.5 billion in losses, including $2.9 billion from business email compromise and over $1.3 billion from impersonation-related scams.
source_2.relevance: Shows that impersonation-based fraud is already widespread and could be exacerbated by voice cloning technologies.

source_3.title: McAfee Artificial Imposters Report (2023)
source_3.key_finding: Voice cloning tools can generate highly convincing voice replicas from very short audio samples, with as little as three seconds producing approximately 85% similarity, with higher accuracy achievable using additional data.
source_3.relevance: Indicates that the technical barrier to voice cloning is extremely low, meaning VoiceSync’s 20-second enrolment requirement presents a significant dual-use risk.
